The following stories on housing issues are available in IRE's Resource Center

November 2000
Investigative MethodsBusiness & EconomyGovernment & Politics

IRE's Resource Center offers several investigative journalism pieces on housing issues, including The Seattle Times' housing affordability series and investigations into corruption and inadequate oversight in public and private housing systems.

The following stories on housing issues are available in IRE's Resource Center:

The Seattle Times' series, "Sticker Shocked," can be seen online at http://seattletimes.nwsource.com/homes/ or it is available in the IRE Resource Center, story #16511.

Story #15321. WWOR-TV investigates the New York City Housing Authority, the country's largest landlord, and finds that tax-funded maintenance money lined the pockets of a few housing inspectors.

Story #14992. Northwestern uncovers a housing inspection system where landlords with the right resources could bypass city housing codes.
